description: Ohio's largest native bee — a wood-boring soloist that pollinates with its whole body through buzz pollination, visiting a wide range of flowers from spring through fall.
icon:
<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="15" rx="4" ry="5.5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.35"/>
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="9.5" rx="3.5" ry="3" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.6"/>
        <ellipse cx="6.5" cy="10" rx="5.5" ry="3" transform="rotate(-18 6.5 10)" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.25"/>
        <ellipse cx="17.5" cy="10" rx="5.5" ry="3" transform="rotate(18 17.5 10)" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.25"/>
        <path d="M10 7.5 C8 5.5 6 3.5 5 2" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.2" stroke-linecap="round"/>
        <path d="M14 7.5 C16 5.5 18 3.5 19 2" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.2" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      </svg>

description: A tiny, often dazzlingly metallic-green native bee that pollinates a wide range of flowers — and now and then lands to sip the salt from your skin. Most nest in bare ground, and as a family they run the full range from solitary to social.
icon:
<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="14.5" rx="3.4" ry="5.2" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.4"/>
        <path d="M8.7 12.6 H15.3 M8.6 15.4 H15.4 M9 18 H15" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.55"/>
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="8.8" rx="2.8" ry="2.4" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.6"/>
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="5.4" rx="2.2" ry="1.8" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.55"/>
        <ellipse cx="6.6" cy="9.5" rx="5.2" ry="2.2" transform="rotate(-22 6.6 9.5)" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.22"/>
        <ellipse cx="17.4" cy="9.5" rx="5.2" ry="2.2" transform="rotate(22 17.4 9.5)" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.22"/>
        <path d="M10.8 4.3 C10 3.4 9.4 3 8.8 2.7" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.5"/>
        <path d="M13.2 4.3 C14 3.4 14.6 3 15.2 2.7" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.5"/>
      </svg>

description: Ohio's big, fuzzy, social bees — black-and-gold buzz-pollinators that vibrate flowers to shake pollen loose, fly in cool weather many bees can't, and live in annual colonies founded each spring by a single overwintered queen.
icon:
<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="14.5" rx="4.6" ry="5.6" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.42"/>
        <path d="M7.6 13 H16.4 M7.8 16.2 H16.2" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.3" opacity="0.5"/>
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="8.6" rx="3.4" ry="2.9" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.62"/>
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="5" rx="2.2" ry="1.8" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.55"/>
        <ellipse cx="6" cy="9.4" rx="5.6" ry="2.4" transform="rotate(-20 6 9.4)" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.22"/>
        <ellipse cx="18" cy="9.4" rx="5.6" ry="2.4" transform="rotate(20 18 9.4)" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.22"/>
        <path d="M10.7 3.9 C9.9 3 9.3 2.6 8.7 2.3" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.5"/>
        <path d="M13.3 3.9 C14.1 3 14.7 2.6 15.3 2.3" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.5"/>
      </svg>

description: Robust, fast, tawny native bees of late-summer flowers — the males marked by remarkably long antennae. Many are devoted to composites like sunflowers and coneflowers, nesting in bare ground and timing their flight to the bloom.
icon:
<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="15" rx="3.6" ry="5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.42"/>
        <path d="M8.6 13.6 H15.4 M8.8 16.6 H15.2" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.1" opacity="0.5"/>
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="9.8" rx="3" ry="2.6" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.6"/>
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="6.6" rx="1.9" ry="1.6" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.55"/>
        <ellipse cx="6.4" cy="10.6" rx="5" ry="2.1" transform="rotate(-22 6.4 10.6)" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.2"/>
        <ellipse cx="17.6" cy="10.6" rx="5" ry="2.1" transform="rotate(22 17.6 10.6)" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.2"/>
        <path d="M11 5.6 C9.6 4 8.4 2.8 7.4 1.6" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.62"/>
        <path d="M13 5.6 C14.4 4 15.6 2.8 16.6 1.6" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.62"/>
      </svg>

description: A harmless fly in bee's colours — the adults are steady pollinators, while the aphid-eating larvae of many species quietly keep pests in check. Tell it from a bee by its single pair of wings and the hovering flight.
icon:
<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="15" rx="2.6" ry="5.2" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.4"/>
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="9.5" rx="2.8" ry="2.4" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.6"/>
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="5.8" rx="2.7" ry="1.9" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.55"/>
        <ellipse cx="6" cy="9" rx="6" ry="1.9" transform="rotate(-10 6 9)" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.22"/>
        <ellipse cx="18" cy="9" rx="6" ry="1.9" transform="rotate(10 18 9)" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.22"/>
      </svg>

description: The garden's favourite predator — adults and their alligator-like larvae both devour aphids. The familiar one is now often the introduced Asian species, while several natives, like the nine-spotted, have grown scarce.
icon:
<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
        <circle cx="12" cy="13" r="6.2" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.4"/>
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="6.8" rx="2.4" ry="1.8" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.6"/>
        <path d="M12 7.5 L12 19" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.55"/>
        <circle cx="9.4" cy="11.5" r="1" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.6"/>
        <circle cx="14.6" cy="11.5" r="1" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.6"/>
        <circle cx="9.2" cy="15.4" r="1" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.6"/>
        <circle cx="14.8" cy="15.4" r="1" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.6"/>
      </svg>

description: A beetle that makes cold light — its dusk-time flashes are a courtship code, and its predatory "glowworm" larvae hunt soft-bodied prey in damp litter. A sensitive sign of dark, undisturbed ground.
icon:
<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="10.5" rx="2.6" ry="5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.4"/>
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="6" rx="2.4" ry="2" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.6"/>
        <circle cx="12" cy="17.5" r="4" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.18"/>
        <circle cx="12" cy="17.5" r="2.3" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.65"/>
        <path d="M9 4.5 C8 3.5 7.2 3 6.4 2.6" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.5"/>
        <path d="M15 4.5 C16 3.5 16.8 3 17.6 2.6" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.5"/>
      </svg>

description: The frail, golden-eyed lacewing of summer dusk — its delicate adults often sip nectar and honeydew, while its spindle-shaped larvae, the "aphid lions," hunt aphids and other soft-bodied prey with sickle jaws.
icon:
<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="13" rx="1.7" ry="5.4" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.55"/>
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="6.4" rx="1.9" ry="1.7" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.6"/>
        <ellipse cx="6.6" cy="11" rx="6" ry="2.6" transform="rotate(-22 6.6 11)" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.3"/>
        <ellipse cx="17.4" cy="11" rx="6" ry="2.6" transform="rotate(22 17.4 11)" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.3"/>
        <ellipse cx="7.6" cy="15.2" rx="5" ry="2.1" transform="rotate(-14 7.6 15.2)" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.22"/>
        <ellipse cx="16.4" cy="15.2" rx="5" ry="2.1" transform="rotate(14 16.4 15.2)" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.22"/>
        <path d="M11 5.2 C10 4 9 3.2 8.2 2.8" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.5"/>
        <path d="M13 5.2 C14 4 15 3.2 15.8 2.8" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.5"/>
      </svg>

description: The maligned little herd at the base of the food web — soft sap-feeders that can stress a plant in numbers, yet feed ladybugs, lacewings, and hover fly larvae. The red ones on false sunflower are most likely native Uroleucon.
icon:
<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="13" rx="4" ry="5.2" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.4"/>
        <ellipse cx="12" cy="7.5" rx="1.9" ry="1.7" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.6"/>
        <path d="M11 6.4 C10 5 9 4 8.2 3.2" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.5"/>
        <path d="M13 6.4 C14 5 15 4 15.8 3.2" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.5"/>
        <path d="M9.4 16.5 C8.8 17.8 8.4 18.6 8.2 19.4" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.55"/>
        <path d="M14.6 16.5 C15.2 17.8 15.6 18.6 15.8 19.4" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.1" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.55"/>
      </svg>
